topic: Upload per-platform deep dive — auth flows, rate limits, content specs, error handling for TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook

focus: Platform API Survey -- TikTok Content Posting API & YouTube Data API v3
noveltyJustification: 5 of 6 findings contain new detailed information not in gen1 context (exact endpoints, token expiry times, quota costs, rate limits, API comparison); Finding 6 is gap identification (0.5 credit). (5 + 0.5*1) / 6 = 0.83

focus: Instagram Graph API & Facebook Video API -- Platform Survey Completion
noveltyJustification: 7.5 of 8 findings are entirely new (Instagram container flow, content types, video specs, rate limits, Facebook Reels 3-phase flow, FB Reels specs, FB video upload, Meta token lifecycle). Finding 8 partially overlaps gen1 token knowledge (+0.5). (7 + 0.5*1) / 8 = 0.94

focus: Video Content Specs (Q5) & Rate Limits Deep Dive (Q6)
noveltyJustification: Findings 1-3 fully new (TikTok specs, YouTube specs, YouTube bitrates), Findings 4-5 partially new (TikTok rate details expanded, YouTube rate mechanism clarified), Findings 6-7 redundant (confirming iter 2), Findings 8-9 new synthesis. (5 + 0.5*2 + 0*2) / 9 = 0.67 + 0.05 simplification bonus for unified matrices = 0.72
ruledOut: TikTok Direct Post API reference for video specs (use Media Transfer Guide instead)
Standardized X-RateLimit-* headers across platforms (none use them)

noveltyJustification: Findings 1-4 fully new (TikTok error catalog, YouTube error catalog, YouTube retry infra, Meta error system) = 4 new. Finding 5 is new synthesis (unified retry design) = 1 new. Findings 6-7 partially new (token lifecycle expands iter 1-2 auth data, adds rotation policies and n8n handling) = 2 x 0.5. (5 + 0.5*2) / 7 = 0.86 minus 0.07 for YouTube resume URI gap = 0.79
ruledOut: Instagram-specific error codes separate from Meta Graph API (same system)
YouTube resume URI persistence docs in upload guide (abstracted by client lib)

focus: Platform-specific quirks (Q9) and n8n integration patterns (Q10)
noveltyJustification: Findings 1-3 fully new (TikTok 4-layer duplicate detection, IG container 24h expiry, YouTube processing states with all enum values) = 3 new. Findings 4-5 fully new (n8n OAuth2 architecture, n8n error handling with critical retry bug) = 2 new. Finding 6 new (n8n community nodes assessment) = 1 new. Finding 7 partially new (FB encoding synthesis from prior data) = 0.5. (6 + 0.5*1 + 0*0) / 7 = 0.93 minus 0.14 for 2 sources blocked (403) reducing corroboration = 0.79
ruledOut: TikTok duplicate detection via API error codes (detection is post-upload moderation, not API-level)
n8n built-in TikTok node (does not exist, community node abandoned)
n8n Retry On Fail combined with Continue Error Output (settings conflict, retries silently ignored)

focus: Upload queue architecture (Q11) and Multi-account management (Q12)
noveltyJustification: Findings 1-4 are new architectural designs synthesized from prior data (queue state machine, priority scheduling, retry backoff, rate limit tracker table) = 4 fully new. Finding 5 partially new (multi-account relationships clarified + encryption approach new, but token storage per platform repeats iter 1-2) = 0.5. Finding 6 new (token refresh automation workflow) = 1. Finding 7 partially new (n8n credential details expand iter 5, dual-storage sync is new) = 0.5. (5 + 0.5*2) / 7 = 0.86 minus 0.15 for n8n doc fetch failures reducing corroboration = 0.71
ruledOut: n8n webhook trigger for queue processing (bypasses priority ordering and rate limit checks)
n8n built-in retry for upload error handling (Retry On Fail bug confirmed again)

focus: Formal closure of Q1-Q4 -- sub-gap fill (account types, scheduling, content types, regular video upload)
noveltyJustification: Findings 1,3,5,7 fully new (TikTok audit restrictions, YouTube publishAt details, IG content type matrix, FB regular video flow) = 4 new. Finding 2 partially new (TikTokAutoUploader expands gen1 with detection vectors) = 0.5. Finding 4 partially new (n8n YouTube node capabilities consolidate prior data + add quota increase timeline) = 0.5. Finding 6 new but thin (IG Business vs Creator = no difference) = 0.5. Finding 8 new (FB scheduling limitations) = 1. (5.5 + 0.5*1) / 8 = 0.69 after rounding minus simplification of account-type non-distinctions
ruledOut: TikTok Creator vs Business account API differences (no difference exists)
Facebook regular video scheduling via Graph API (not available)
